questions.text: Two charges q1 and q2 are placed in vacuum at a distance d and force between them is F. If a medium of relative permittivity 4 is introduced between them then new force will be:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The force between two charges in a medium is given by Coulomb’s law, which states that the force is inversely proportional to the permittivity of the medium. If the permittivity of the medium increases, the force decreases.In vacuum, F=k⋅q1⋅q2/​d2wherek is Coulomb’s constant.When a medium with relative permittivity εr​=4 F′=F​/εr ( Fmed=Fvac/εr ) ​=F​/4So, the new force will be one-fourth of the original force.
Incorrect as per formula: F=k⋅q1⋅q2/​d2 Fmed=Fvac/εr
Incorrect as per formula: F=k⋅q1⋅q2/​d2 Fmed=Fvac/εr
Incorrect as per formula: F=k⋅q1⋅q2/​d2 Fmed=Fvac/εr

questions.explanation: The force between two charges in a medium is given by Coulomb’s law, which states that the force is inversely proportional to the permittivity of the medium. If the permittivity of the medium increases, the force decreases.In vacuum, F=k⋅q1⋅q2/​d2wherek is Coulomb’s constant.When a medium with relative permittivity εr​=4 F′=F​/εr ( Fmed=Fvac/εr ) ​=F​/4So, the new force will be one-fourth of the original force.
hints: Two charges q1 and q2 are placed in vacuum

questions.text: Three capacitors of 4μF each are connected in such a way that net capacitance of their combination is 6μF. It is possible if:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: It is incorrect as per formula and condition: 1/Cseries=1/C1​+1/C2​+1/C3 =1/4μF​+1/4μF​+1/4μF​ =3/4μF​
It is incorrect as per formula and condition: Cparallel​=C1​+C2+C3 =4μF+4μF+4μF =12μF

options.text: Two in series and one in parallel
options.explanation: If two capacitors are connected in series and the resulting combination is in parallel with the third capacitor,Two capacitors in series: 1/Cseries=1/C1​+1/C2​ =1/4μF+1/4μF =1/2μF Cseries=2μFThe resulting combination in series with the third capacitor: Ctotal​​=Cseries+C3​ ​=2μF​+4μF​ =6μF​So, the total capacitance is 6μF. Therefore, it is possible if two capacitors are connected in series and the resulting combination is in parallel with the third capacitor.

options.text: Two in parallel and one in series
options.explanation: If two capacitors are connected in parallel and the resulting combination is in series with the third capacitor,Two capacitors in parallel: Cparallel​=C1​+C2​ =4μF+4μF =8μFThe resulting combination in series with the third capacitor: 1/Ctotal​​=1/Cparallel​+1/C3​ ​=1/8μF​+1/4μF​ =3/8μF​So, the total capacitance is 3/8μF. Therefore, it is incorrect as per condition.

questions.explanation: If two capacitors are connected in series and the resulting combination is in parallel with the third capacitor,Two capacitors in series: 1/Cseries=1/C1​+1/C2​ =1/4μF+1/4μF =1/2μF Cseries=2μFThe resulting combination in series with the third capacitor: Ctotal​​=Cseries+C3​ ​=2μF​+4μF​ =6μF​So, the total capacitance is 6μF. Therefore, it is possible if two capacitors are connected in series and the resulting combination is in parallel with the third capacitor.
hints: net capacitance of their combination is 6μF

questions.text: Slope of I-V graph of a resistor is numerically equal to:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The slope of the current-voltage (I-V) graph for a resistor is numerically equal to the reciprocal of the resistance (conductivity) (1/R) of the resistor. This is derived from Ohm’s law, which states that the current (I) through a conductor between two points is directly proportional to the voltage (V) across the two points, and inversely proportional to the resistance (R). It is represented as: I=V/R​So, when you plot I-V graph, the slope of the line is 1/R.
Incorrect as per formula and condition: I=V/R​So, when you plot I-V graph, the slope of the line is 1/R.﻿
Incorrect as per formula and condition: I=V/R​So, when you plot I-V graph, the slope of the line is 1/R.
Incorrect as per formula and condition: I=V/R​So, when you plot I-V graph, the slope of the line is 1/R.

questions.explanation: The slope of the current-voltage (I-V) graph for a resistor is numerically equal to the reciprocal of the resistance (conductivity) (1/R) of the resistor. This is derived from Ohm’s law, which states that the current (I) through a conductor between two points is directly proportional to the voltage (V) across the two points, and inversely proportional to the resistance (R). It is represented as: I=V/R​So, when you plot I-V graph, the slope of the line is 1/R.

questions.text: Which one of the following is correct for an open circuit?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: No, that’s not correct. In an open circuit, the terminal voltage (Vt) is equal to the source voltage, not zero. This is because there is no current flow in an open circuit, and hence, no voltage drop across any internal resistance of the source. Therefore, the entire source voltage appears across the open-circuited terminals.

No, that’s not correct. In an open circuit, the terminal voltage (Vt) is equal to the source voltage (E), not less than it. This is because there is no current flow in an open circuit, and hence, no voltage drop across any internal resistance of the source. Therefore, the entire source voltage appears across the open-circuited terminals. So, we have Vt = E in an open circuit.

In an open circuit, the terminal voltage (Vt) is equal to the source voltage (E). This is because there is no current flow in an open circuit, and hence, no voltage drop across any internal resistance of the source. Therefore, the entire source voltage appears across the open-circuited terminals. So, we have Vt = E in an open circuit.

The equation Vt = E - Ir is known as the terminal voltage equation, where Vt is the terminal voltage, E is the electromotive force (or source voltage), I is the current, and r is the internal resistance.However, in an open circuit, the current I is zero because there is no path for the current to flow. Therefore, the term Ir becomes zero, and the equation simplifies to Vt = E.

questions.explanation: In an open circuit, the terminal voltage (Vt) is equal to the source voltage (E). This is because there is no current flow in an open circuit, and hence, no voltage drop across any internal resistance of the source. Therefore, the entire source voltage appears across the open-circuited terminals. So, we have Vt = E in an open circuit.

questions.text: Magnetic flux will be maximum when:
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Magnetic field is perpendicular to the plane area
options.explanation: When the magnetic field is perpendicular to the plane of the area (i.e., the angle θ between the magnetic field and the normal to the surface is 0°), the magnetic flux is maximized. This is because the cos of 0° is 1, and from the magnetic flux equation ΦB​=BAcosθ, we can see that the flux ΦB is maximum when cos θ is maximum (which is 1). So, for maximum magnetic flux, the magnetic field should be perpendicular to the plane of the area.

options.text: Magnetic field lies parallel to the plane area
options.explanation: Incorrect as per formula: ΦB​=BAcosθ

options.text: Area is held at an angle of 45º
options.explanation: Incorrect as per formula: ΦB​=BAcosθ

options.text: Magnetic flux density is perpendicular to area vector of the surface
options.explanation: Incorrect as per formula: ΦB​=BAcosθ

questions.explanation: When the magnetic field is perpendicular to the plane of the area (i.e., the angle θ between the magnetic field and the normal to the surface is 0°), the magnetic flux is maximized. This is because the cos of 0° is 1, and from the magnetic flux equation ΦB​=BAcosθ, we can see that the flux ΦB is maximum when cos θ is maximum (which is 1). So, for maximum magnetic flux, the magnetic field should be perpendicular to the plane of the area.

questions.text: A proton is moving along the axis of a solenoid carrying a current. The magnetic force on proton will be:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect as per given condition.
Incorrect as per given condition.

The correct option for the question is Zero. When a proton is moving along the axis of a solenoid, the magnetic force on the proton is indeed zero. This is because the angle between the direction of motion and the magnetic field is 0 degrees, and the sine of 0 degrees is zero. Therefore, there is no magnetic force acting on the proton.
Incorrect as per given condition.

This is because the angle between the direction of motion and the magnetic field is 0 degrees, and the sine of 0 degrees is zero. Therefore, there is no magnetic force acting on the proton.

questions.text: A steady current is passing through a coil, magnitude of self- induced emf in it, will be:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The magnitude of self-induced electromotive force (emf) in a coil, also known as back emf, is given by Faraday’s law of electromagnetic induction. The law states that the induced emf is equal to the negative rate of change of magnetic flux through the coil.However, if a steady current is passing through the coil, the magnetic field produced by the coil is constant. This means that the magnetic flux through the coil is not changing with time. Therefore, the rate of change of magnetic flux is zero.In mathematical terms, this can be expressed as: emf=− ΔΦ/​Δtwhere:emf is the induced electromotive force,Φ is the magnetic flux, andt is time.Since ΔΦ/​Δt=0 for a steady current (because the magnetic flux Φ is not changing with time), the self-induced emf will be zero.So, the magnitude of self-induced emf in a coil with a steady current passing through it will be zero.
Incorrect as per formula and condition: emf=− ΔΦ/​Δt
Incorrect as per formula and condition: emf=− ΔΦ/​Δt
Incorrect as per formula and condition: emf=− ΔΦ/​Δt

questions.explanation: The magnitude of self-induced electromotive force (emf) in a coil, also known as back emf, is given by Faraday’s law of electromagnetic induction. The law states that the induced emf is equal to the negative rate of change of magnetic flux through the coil.However, if a steady current is passing through the coil, the magnetic field produced by the coil is constant. This means that the magnetic flux through the coil is not changing with time. Therefore, the rate of change of magnetic flux is zero.In mathematical terms, this can be expressed as: emf=− ΔΦ/​Δtwhere:emf is the induced electromotive force,Φ is the magnetic flux, andt is time.Since ΔΦ/​Δt=0 for a steady current (because the magnetic flux Φ is not changing with time), the self-induced emf will be zero.So, the magnitude of self-induced emf in a coil with a steady current passing through it will be zero.

questions.text: At high frequency in a capacitive circuit, the current will be:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: In a capacitive circuit, as the frequency increases, the capacitive reactance (Xc) decreases. The capacitive reactance is given by the formula:Xc=1/2πfCwhere:(Xc) is the capacitive reactance,(f) is the frequency, and(C) is the capacitance.Since the current (I) in the circuit is given by (I = V/Xc) (where (V) is the voltage), as (Xc) decreases with increasing frequency, the current (I) will increase.So, at high frequency in a capacitive circuit, the current will be large.
Incorrect as per given condition and formula: Xc=1/2πfC I = V/Xc
Incorrect as per given condition and formula: Xc=1/2πfC I = V/Xc
Incorrect as per given condition and formula: Xc=1/2πfC I = V/Xc

questions.explanation: In a capacitive circuit, as the frequency increases, the capacitive reactance (Xc) decreases. The capacitive reactance is given by the formula:Xc=1/2πfCwhere:(Xc) is the capacitive reactance,(f) is the frequency, and(C) is the capacitance.Since the current (I) in the circuit is given by (I = V/Xc) (where (V) is the voltage), as (Xc) decreases with increasing frequency, the current (I) will increase.So, at high frequency in a capacitive circuit, the current will be large.

questions.text: The peak value of A.C source is 20 A, its rms value will be:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The root mean square (rms) value of an alternating current (AC) can be calculated from its peak value using the following formula: Irms​ = ​Ipeak ​/√2where:(Irms) is the rms value of the current,(Ipeak) is the peak value of the current.Given that the peak value (Ipeak) is 20 A, we can substitute this into the formula to find the rms value: Irms​ = ​Ipeak ​/√2 =​20A/√2 ​=14.1ASo, the rms value of the AC source is approximately 14.1A.
Incorrect as per given condition and formula: Irms​ = ​Ipeak ​/√2
Incorrect as per given condition and formula: Irms​ = ​Ipeak ​/√2
Incorrect as per given condition and formula: Irms​ = ​Ipeak ​/√2

questions.explanation: The root mean square (rms) value of an alternating current (AC) can be calculated from its peak value using the following formula: Irms​ = ​Ipeak ​/√2where:(Irms) is the rms value of the current,(Ipeak) is the peak value of the current.Given that the peak value (Ipeak) is 20 A, we can substitute this into the formula to find the rms value: Irms​ = ​Ipeak ​/√2 =​20A/√2 ​=14.1ASo, the rms value of the AC source is approximately 14.1A.

questions.text: The materials in which disordered structure of liquid is frozen are:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The materials in which the disordered structure of a liquid is frozen are typically referred to as amorphous solids. These materials do not have a long-range ordered structure like crystals, but instead, their atomic or molecular arrangements are disordered, similar to the structure of a liquid. One common example of such materials is glass.
Crystalline materials have atoms, ions, or molecules arranged in a highly ordered, repeating pattern extending in all three spatial dimensions
Polycrystalline materials are composed of many small crystals or grains of varying size and orientation.
Quartz is a hard, crystalline mineral composed of silica (silicon dioxide). The atoms are linked in a continuous framework of SiO4 silicon–oxygen tetrahedra.

questions.explanation: The materials in which the disordered structure of a liquid is frozen are typically referred to as amorphous solids. These materials do not have a long-range ordered structure like crystals, but instead, their atomic or molecular arrangements are disordered, similar to the structure of a liquid. One common example of such materials is glass.
hints: disordered structure of liquid is frozen

questions.text: The temperature below which the resistivity of a superconductor falls to zero is called
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This is a temperature measurement made relative to absolute zero, the lowest possible temperature. Absolute zero is the point at which all molecular motion ceases.
This is the unit of absolute temperature in the International System of Units (SI).
This term is often used in thermodynamics to refer to the highest or lowest temperature achievable in a given system or process.

The temperature below which the resistivity of a superconductor falls to zero is called the critical temperature or transition temperature, often denoted as Tc.​Below this temperature, the material exhibits zero electrical resistance, becoming a superconductor. Above Tc the material behaves as a normal conductor.

questions.explanation: The temperature below which the resistivity of a superconductor falls to zero is called the critical temperature or transition temperature, often denoted as Tc.​Below this temperature, the material exhibits zero electrical resistance, becoming a superconductor. Above Tc the material behaves as a normal conductor.

questions.text: In a half wave rectifier, the diode conducts:
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Only positive half of input cycle
options.explanation: In a half-wave rectifier, the diode conducts during the only positive half of the input cycle. During the negative half of the input cycle, the diode is reverse biased and does not conduct. Therefore, only the positive half of the AC input is converted to DC output. The negative half of the input is blocked. This is the fundamental operation of a half-wave rectifier.

options.text: Only negative half of input cycle
options.explanation: Incorrect as per given statement.
Incorrect as per given statement.

options.text: Any one half of input cycle
options.explanation: Incorrect as per given statement.

questions.explanation: In a half-wave rectifier, the diode conducts during the only positive half of the input cycle. During the negative half of the input cycle, the diode is reverse biased and does not conduct. Therefore, only the positive half of the AC input is converted to DC output. The negative half of the input is blocked. This is the fundamental operation of a half-wave rectifier.

questions.text: The maximum kinetic energy of emitted photoelectrons, from different metal surfaces, depends upon:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect as per given condition and formula: K.Emax = hf-ΦThe intensity of the incident light affects the number of photoelectrons emitted but not their maximum kinetic energy. Even if the intensity of the light is increased, it will not increase the energy of individual photons; instead, it will increase the number of photons.

options.text: Frequency of the incident light only
options.explanation: Incorrect as per given condition and formula: K.Emax = hf-Φ
Incorrect as per given condition and formula: K.Emax = hf-Φ

options.text: Both frequency of incident light and nature of metal surface
options.explanation: The maximum kinetic energy of emitted photoelectrons depends on both the frequency of the incident light and the nature of the metal surface. As: K.Emax = hf-Φwhere,h= Plank's connstantf= frequencyΦ= work function (different metals has different work function so K.Emax also depend on metal nature)This means that the photoelectron’s kinetic energy is the energy supplied by the photon, minus the energy used to overcome the work function.

questions.explanation: The maximum kinetic energy of emitted photoelectrons depends on both the frequency of the incident light and the nature of the metal surface. As: K.Emax = hf-Φwhere,h= Plank's connstantf= frequencyΦ= work function (different metals has different work function so K.Emax also depend on metal nature)This means that the photoelectron’s kinetic energy is the energy supplied by the photon, minus the energy used to overcome the work function.
hints: maximum kinetic energy of emitted photoelectrons

questions.text: The rest mass of photon is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect according to the theory of relativity.

The rest mass of a photon is 0. This is because photons are particles of light and they always move at the speed of light in a vacuum. According to the theory of relativity, any particle that moves at the speed of light must have zero rest mass. Therefore, the rest mass of a photon is 0.m0​=0where (m0) is the rest mass of the photon.
Incorrect according to the theory of relativity.
Incorrect according to the theory of relativity.

questions.explanation: The rest mass of a photon is 0. This is because photons are particles of light and they always move at the speed of light in a vacuum. According to the theory of relativity, any particle that moves at the speed of light must have zero rest mass. Therefore, the rest mass of a photon is 0.m0​=0where (m0) is the rest mass of the photon.

questions.text: The type of spectra produced by atoms is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: A continuous spectrum contains all wavelengths of light in a certain range.

Band spectra, also known as molecular spectra, are produced by molecules radiating their rotational or vibrational energies, or both simultaneously. Band spectra are groups of lines so closely spaced that each group appears to be a band.

Atoms produce line spectra. This is because atoms emit light at only specific wavelengths, producing a line spectrum rather than the continuous spectrum of all wavelengths produced by a hot object. Bohr’s model suggests that the atomic spectra of atoms are produced by electrons gaining energy from some source, jumping up to a higher energy level, then immediately dropping back to a lower energy level and emitting the energy difference between the two energy levels. This results in emission at specific wavelengths, or lines, hence the term “line spectra”.

Bremsstrahlung, or braking radiation, is electromagnetic radiation produced by the deceleration of a charged particle when deflected by another charged particle, typically an electron by an atomic nucleus. Bremsstrahlung has a continuous spectrum, which becomes more intense and whose peak intensity shifts toward higher frequencies as the change of the energy of the decelerated particles increases.

questions.explanation: Atoms produce line spectra. This is because atoms emit light at only specific wavelengths, producing a line spectrum rather than the continuous spectrum of all wavelengths produced by a hot object. Bohr’s model suggests that the atomic spectra of atoms are produced by electrons gaining energy from some source, jumping up to a higher energy level, then immediately dropping back to a lower energy level and emitting the energy difference between the two energy levels. This results in emission at specific wavelengths, or lines, hence the term “line spectra”.

questions.text: The half-life of a radioactive element which has only 1/32 of its original mass left after elapsed of 60 days is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect as per formula and given condition: N=N0​×(1/2)t/T
Incorrect as per formula and given condition: N=N0​×(1/2)t/T
Incorrect as per formula and given condition: N=N0​×(1/2)t/T

As: N=N0​×(1/2)t/TGiven that N/N0​=1/32 and t=60 days, we can substitute these values into the equation: 1/32=(1/2)60/TTaking the natural logarithm of both sides gives: ln(1/32)=60/T ​ln(1/2)Solving for T gives: T=60 ln(1/2)​/ln(1/32) =60(-0.693)/(-3.465) =60(0.2) =12So, the half-life of the radioactive element is 12 days.

questions.explanation: As: N=N0​×(1/2)t/TGiven that N/N0​=1/32 and t=60 days, we can substitute these values into the equation: 1/32=(1/2)60/TTaking the natural logarithm of both sides gives: ln(1/32)=60/T ​ln(1/2)Solving for T gives: T=60 ln(1/2)​/ln(1/32) =60(-0.693)/(-3.465) =60(0.2) =12So, the half-life of the radioactive element is 12 days.
